name: Gnocchi Di Potate
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and chop the potatoes into evenly sized pieces.
2. Cook the potatoes until soft. They should still be warm.
3. Place the potatoes in a potato ricer or mash them with a fork. You can also use a food processor to mash them.
4. Add the potatoes to a large bowl and season with salt.
5. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups of flour, a little at a time, and mix well with your hands until the dough is smooth, pliable, and slightly sticky.
6. Transfer the mixture to a wooden board and knead lightly, adding more flour only if needed. Knead for about 2-3 minutes until it is smooth, pliable, and slightly sticky.
7. Divide the dough into several equal pieces, about the size of an orange.
8. Lightly dust your hands with flour (do not dust the work area, or the dough will not slide smoothly).
9. Using both hands, roll out each piece of dough into a thin ribbon using a light back-and-forth motion. The thickness should be about the width of your index finger.
10. Hold a fork against the work surface with the curved part facing away from you.
11. Starting from the curved outside bottom of the fork, press each strip of dough firmly upward along the length of the tines.
12. Allow the gnocchi to fall back onto the work surface.
13. Repeat steps 10-12 until all pieces are shaped into gnocchi.
14. Place the gnocchi on a lightly floured platter or cookie sheet. They can be cooked immediately or kept in the refrigerator, uncovered, for several hours or overnight.
15. Serve with your favorite sauce.
PS: I still remember when I was a little girl, my mother would always give me the fork to shape the gnocchi.
